page: OVHcloud c3-16 is a compute-optimized virtual server powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated physical cores on the x86_64 architecture, utilizing an AMD EPYC Milan processor running at 2.3 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a ratio of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is provided by a 200 GB NVMe SSD, and the network interface supports a baseline bandwidth of 1 Gbps with one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance, Geekbench single-core scores, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression all placing in the top 25%. Multi-core performance, including stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core metrics, falls within the average range. Memory bandwidth tests for both cached small blocks and uncached large blocks also demonstrate average performance. For specialized workloads, the server achieves strong results in text processing and text generation for small language models, while medium model inference and database operations remain in the average tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated core allocation and NVMe storage provide a predictable resource profile for compute-bound tasks without the overhead of hyperthreading. However, the 2.0 GB of RAM per core represents a tradeoff for memory-intensive applications. This server is configured for workloads such as single-threaded processing, software compilation, text processing, and lightweight web serving.
description: The OVHcloud c3-16 is a compute-optimized virtual server featuring 8 dedicated physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC Milan processor running at 2.3 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM, a 200 GB NVMe SSD, and a 1 Gbps baseline network interface. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, this instance delivers strong single-core performance, as evidenced by top-25% results in stress-ng and Geekbench single-core benchmarks, alongside strong single-threaded bzip3 decompression and text processing. Multi-core performance and memory bandwidth metrics remain in the average tier. With a resource ratio of 2.0 GB of RAM per core, the server offers a balanced profile for compute-bound tasks but presents a tradeoff for memory-heavy applications. It is suited for software compilation, text processing, lightweight web serving, and single-threaded computational workloads.
og_description: OVHcloud c3-16 is a compute-optimized server with 8 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan cores, 16 GB RAM, and 200 GB NVMe storage, delivering strong single-core performance for compute-bound workloads.

tagline: A compute-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC Milan cores and local NVMe storage for single-threaded and compute-bound workloads.

16.0 GB of system memory and 200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Strong single-core CPU and single-threaded decompression performance
1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with one public IPv4 address
